title: how to cache  layer file when  `skopeo`  copy from docker reigstry to local dir?

comments.body: Why not copy it to container/storage?

> Why not copy it to container/storage?

~, i want docker save the images from public docker registry to a tar.gz file,  and dispatch this file to private docker registry which has no interenet access.

and i see the options ` --dest-shared-blob-dir` can cache the blob?

`dir:` will not download a blob if it already exists in the destination directory.

> and i see the options `--dest-shared-blob-dir` can cache the blob?

As documented, that applies only to the OCI transports.

----

`dir:` is primarily a debugging mechanism; for transporting a large number of images, I’d generally recommend setting up a temporary registry (perhaps running in a container, and almost certainly `localhost`-only), copying images there (benefiting from all the within-repo and cross-repo sharing mechanisms that already exist), copying images to that registry; then transferring the backing storage of that registry to the destination, and again setting up a temporary registry to copy images from.

As there are Hundreds of images; it is very time cost copy images at every build time~

And why `dir:` is not recommend for use as cache when  transporting a large number of images?  

many thanks!

Basically the code to reuse blobs across repos, and especially to reuse different but equivalent blobs — and to do all of that in parallel with multiple concurrent writers, is non-trivial, and already exists in `docker://`; so it’s not _that_ attractive to reimplement something similar for `dir`.

Meanwhile, the simplicity of `dir` is valuable in itself, for debugging.

> especially to reuse different but equivalent blobs

is there possible the  blobs name(sha256) is same but the content is differenet ?  ie  when multiple writer write same blob may corrupt ?

This happens primarily for differently-compressed version (a different algorithm, different implementation, or just a compression output that is not deterministic).

Copying only one version of such a layer can potentially save quite of both of time and size. (OTOH some users do prefer exact copies.)

---

There’s quite a number of ideas about improving `skopeo sync` in the issues in this repo. Using some on-disk format that doesn’t require a registry but allow registry-like features is not _unreasonable_ — but it’s a thing with a fairly easy workaround, whereas many other features don’t have such an easy alternative. So that means the other features are, at least short-term, more likely to get implemented.
i get. thanks very much for your detailed explains !
